directive: STRONG-PAPER DELIVERY TARGET (conj:tail-lower-bound): the converse must be made CONCRETE. Do NOT assume a completed bounded-chi-square family (assume-the-crux is forbidden). Instead PROVE, from the primitives of def:least-favorable-family H_{n,lambda} (the clipped expit-logit propensity perturbation with amplitudes u_1n,u_0n on the shell/regular cells, plus the regression mean shifts delta_1n,delta_0n), that the four laws P_{s,t} (s,t in {-1,1}^2) (i) lie in W_n^lb, (ii) have pairwise n-sample PRODUCT chi-square bounded by a universal constant < 4, and (iii) have tau-diameter >= c_0 R_n^*. Key computation: each shell/cell perturbation amplitude is scaled by the nuisance rate over the cell mass (delta_1n ~ r_1n/P_X(S_lambda)^{1/2}, u_1n ~ r_en/||e(1-e)1_S||_{L2}, etc.), so the single-shell chi-square contribution is O(n * amplitude^2 * cellmass) = O(n * r^2) which must be balanced at lambda_dagger to a constant via def:near-optimal-threshold; sum the independent treated-shell and control-cell contributions and the propensity-tilt contribution. Verify the product-chi-square tensorization (chi^2 of product <= prod(1+chi^2)-1) and that each per-observation chi-square is O(1/n). If after a genuine attempt a step is NOT closable from these primitives, leave conj:tail-lower-bound in honest RESULT-FORM (status to-prove, the chi-square bound an explicit OPEN obligation) and say which inequality is the obstruction — do NOT assume it.

The current handle fixes a control-mean shift of size delta_0n(lambda) = r_0n /(16 P_X(B_lambda)^(1/2)); because ass:regular-cell-mass gives P_X(B_lambda) bounded below uniformly for lambda <= lambda_0, this creates ATE separation of order r_0n, not of order r_0n r_en. Under any natural completion with locally quadratic likelihood, the associated control-arm product-chi-square scale is n r_0n^2, which need not be bounded on the admissible rate branches. Likewise the shell logit perturbation has u_1n(lambda) approximately r_en / lambda^(1 + kappa/2), so its Bernoulli tilt contributes n r_en^2 / lambda to product chi-square, again uncontrolled by def:near-optimal-threshold. More fundamentally, the class-defining properties ass:estimable-g1, ass:estimable-g0, and ass:estimable-e are existential upper-side estimability statements and do not provide a closure principle showing that the perturbed laws remain in W_n^lb. What can be proved from the current handle is the explicit ATE-diameter formula above; the bounded-divergence completion is an open proof obligation, not a proved consequence of the frozen core.

R_n(lambda)=n^(-1/2)v_kappa(lambda)+n^(-1/2)(lambda^(-1)r_1n+r_0n+lambda^(-3/2)r_en+r_en)+lambda^(kappa/2)r_1n+lambda^(-1)r_1n r_en+r_0n r_en. LITERATURE-GROUNDED GUIDANCE for conj:tail-lower-bound (prove the UNCONDITIONAL lower bound). The standard way these structure-agnostic ATE lower bounds are closed (Jin-Syrgkanis 2024 arXiv:2402.14264; Bonvini-Kennedy-Dukes-Balakrishnan 2024 arXiv:2405.08525; Kennedy-Balakrishnan-Robins-Wasserman 2024; Robins-Li-Mukherjee-Tchetgen-vanderVaart 2017) is the METHOD OF FUZZY HYPOTHESES via the Robins-2009 per-bin Hellinger lemma. ENGINE (make it a reusable lemma): partition into k bins, Rademacher prior lambda_j on bins, two mixtures P-bar=integral P_lambda, Q-bar=integral Q_lambda; per-bin discrepancies delta1 (null spread), delta2 (null-alt gap), delta3 (mixture-mean mismatch); then H^2(P-bar^n,Q-bar^n) <= C n (sum p_j)[n(max p_j)(d1 d2 + d2^2)+d3]. THE KEY MOVE: engineer delta3=0 by making the AVERAGED densities coincide (perturb so first-order/mean terms cancel); then only the SECOND-ORDER product survives and H^2=O(1) by taking k->infinity. This is WHY a lone first-order nuisance perturbation of size r gives NO bound (it has delta3~r^2 -> n r^2 -> infinity, detectable), and only the product r_g r_e (or the n^-1/2 variance) is a valid lower bound. IN-CLASS BY CONSTRUCTION (this dissolves the missing-closure-theorem obstruction): do NOT perturb an abstract class member. WRITE the nuisances EXPLICITLY as ghat +/- (perturbation), so ||g-ghat||=the rate EXACTLY by algebra; the structure-agnostic class only demands those L2 balls, so membership is automatic. PROVE FOUR SEPARATE CHANNELS (each a fuzzy-hypothesis/two-point instance), take the MAX, then OPTIMIZE the clip lambda at lambda_dagger: (4) r_0 r_e [global product]: Bonvini pure-SA construction on the BULK where e is bounded away from 0 (no clipping) - omega-hat +/- r_e bumps in BOTH families, control regression ghat_0 perturbed -/+ r_0 to cancel first order (P-bar=Q-bar, delta3=0); product cross-term ~ r_0 r_e survives. (3) lambda^-1 r_1 r_e [clipped product ON shell]: Jin-Syrgkanis ASYMMETRIC NONLINEAR pairing of (e, g_1) (needed because the clipped DR functional is non-inner-product): m_lambda = mhat[1-(beta/ghat_1) Delta], g_lambda(1)=(mhat/m_lambda)[ghat_1 + alpha Delta], control branch untouched - this exactly cancels first order; LOCALIZE on the shell S_lambda (Kennedy localized fuzzy hypotheses, bins inside S_lambda, mass ~lambda^kappa enters p_j ~ lambda^kappa/k); the clip inflates the influence weight by 1/max(e,lambda)~lambda^-1 so the product is lambda^-1 r_1 r_e. (2) lambda^(kappa/2) r_1 [treated-regression on shell]: a LOCALIZED Le Cam two-point (Kennedy regression-bump), single perturbation of g_1 on S_lambda; on a shell of mass lambda^kappa the bump is seen with prob ~lambda^kappa, so effective detectability is n lambda^kappa r_1^2 and the indistinguishable amplitude is r_1 sqrt(lambda^kappa) = lambda^(kappa/2) r_1. (1) n^-1/2 v_kappa(lambda) [variance/oracle]: KNOWN-nuisance two-point with the inflated clipped-score variance; the shell-variance integral ~ integral_{e<=lambda} e^(kappa-1) e^-2 de ~ lambda^(-(1-kappa)) = v_kappa(lambda)^2, giving n^-1/2 v_kappa(lambda). ASSEMBLE: minimax >= max of the four; at lambda_dagger (R_n(lambda_dagger)<=2R_n^*) the terms balance so max >= (1/4) R_n(lambda_dagger) >= c R_n^*. Add labelled lemmas lem:hellinger-per-bin (the engine), lem:lower-product-bulk, lem:lower-product-shell, lem:lower-treated-shell, lem:lower-variance. CAUTION: the kappa/2 exponent in channel (2) depends on whether r_1 is an L2(P) or L2(P|S_lambda) rate (shell-conditional vs unconditional normalization changes kappa/2 vs kappa) - SANITY-CHECK it against an explicit shell integral with the exact clipped score before asserting it; if it differs, report the corrected exponent as a proposed_definition_change to def:frontier-functional rather than forcing kappa/2. NOTE: target the REDUCED functional R_n^lower = n^-1/2 v_kappa + lambda^(kappa/2) r_1 + lambda^-1 r_1 r_e + r_0 r_e (the extra n^-1/2 (lambda^-1 r_1 + r_0 + ...) terms in the current envelope are FEASIBLE-ESTIMATOR achievability artifacts, first-order, NOT minimax lower bounds); if you prove the lower bound at R_n^lower, also emit a proposed_definition_change separating the matched lower frontier from the (looser) upper envelope, and flag the gap.

The current statement invokes conj:tail-lower-bound as its lower half, but that converse is available only under the extra radius condition $r_{1n}\ge c_{\mathrm{rate}}n^{-1/2}$ together with class nonemptiness. Without importing that regime, the displayed lower bound $c n^{-1/2}$ is false on admissible shrinking-ball branches. Indeed, by Cauchy--Schwarz,
\[
|\tau(P)-E\{\tilde g_1(X)-\tilde g_0(X)\}|\le \|g_1-\tilde g_1\|_{L_2(P_X)}+\|g_0-\tilde g_0\|_{L_2(P_X)}\le r_{1n}+r_{0n}.
\]
If $r_{1n}+r_{0n}=o(n^{-1/2})$, then the class itself restricts $\tau$ to an interval of width $o(n^{-1/2})$, so no universal parametric minimax floor can hold. The honest fix is to restate conj:minimax-frontier under the same lower-bound regime as conj:tail-lower-bound. The upper-achievability and phase-diagram portions are unchanged.
